title: Determinacy predicates: compiler intrinsic
analysis: Currently determined/constrained/undetermined/partially_determined are compiled as normal stdlib FunctionCall nodes and dispatched at runtime through reify-stdlib where they return Value::Undef as stubs. The task is to make them compiler intrinsics: the compiler intercepts these function names, verifies the argument is a bare identifier that resolves to a ValueCellId, and emits a new DeterminacyPredicate { kind, cell } variant of CompiledExprKind instead of FunctionCall. This requires: (1) adding DeterminacyPredicateKind enum and the DeterminacyPredicate variant to CompiledExprKind in reify-types, (2) updating all 6 exhaustive matches on CompiledExprKind (walk, collect_value_refs_inner, remap_entity in expr.rs; eval_expr in reify-expr; collect_body_refs_inner in reify-compiler; assert_no_unresolved in boundary2_producer test), (3) adding the compiler intercept in compile_expr_guarded before args are compiled (since we need the raw AST ident, not a compiled expression), and (4) adding a constructor with content hash tag 16. The eval layer (reify-expr) returns Value::Undef for now since it lacks DeterminacyState access; actual evaluation happens at the reify-eval engine level where snapshot state is available (a separate concern for a future task). Content hash tags 0-15 are already allocated to existing variants; tag 16 is the next available.

prerequisites.description: Add DeterminacyPredicateKind enum (Determined, Undetermined, Constrained, PartiallyDetermined) and DeterminacyPredicate { kind, cell } variant to CompiledExprKind in crates/reify-types/src/expr.rs. Add CompiledExpr::determinacy_predicate() constructor with content hash tag [16, kind_byte]. Update walk() (leaf node — no children), collect_value_refs_inner() (push cell to refs — it is a dependency), and remap_entity() (remap cell.entity like ValueRef). Re-export DeterminacyPredicateKind from crates/reify-types/src/lib.rs.

Update eval_expr() in crates/reify-expr/src/lib.rs to handle DeterminacyPredicate variant — return Value::Undef (eval-layer lacks DeterminacyState access; real evaluation is at engine level with snapshot context).

Update collect_body_refs_inner() in crates/reify-compiler/src/lib.rs to handle DeterminacyPredicate — push cell id to refs. Update assert_no_unresolved() in crates/reify-compiler/tests/boundary2_producer.rs — DeterminacyPredicate is a resolved leaf node (no-op, no children to recurse into).

steps.description: Write failing test compile_determined_param in determinacy_compile_tests.rs: parse 'structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm \n let d = determined(x) }', extract the default_expr for cell 'd', assert it matches CompiledExprKind::DeterminacyPredicate { kind: Determined, cell } where cell == ValueCellId::new('S', 'x'). Also assert result_type == Type::Bool. Uses compile_and_get_expr helper pattern from option_compile_tests.rs.

Add compiler intercept in compile_expr_guarded: in the FunctionCall arm, before arg compilation (after the existing 'some()' intercept), check if name matches determined/undetermined/constrained/partially_determined. If so: verify exactly 1 arg, verify arg is ExprKind::Ident, resolve ident via scope.resolve() to ValueCellId, map name to DeterminacyPredicateKind, emit CompiledExpr::determinacy_predicate(kind, cell). On error (wrong arity, non-ident arg, unresolved name), emit Diagnostic::error with source span and return Literal(Undef, Bool).

Write test compile_all_four_determinacy_kinds: structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm, let a = determined(x), let b = undetermined(x), let c = constrained(x), let d = partially_determined(x) }. Extract default_expr for each let and assert each has the correct DeterminacyPredicateKind (Determined, Undetermined, Constrained, PartiallyDetermined) and all reference the same cell ValueCellId::new('S', 'x').
Verify all four kinds pass (should already work from step-2 implementation). Fix any issues.

Write test error_wrong_arity: compile 'structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm 
 let d = determined() }' and 'structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm 
 param y : Scalar = 3mm 
 let d = determined(x, y) }'. Assert each produces at least one error diagnostic containing 'exactly 1 argument'. Assert the let 'd' does NOT compile to DeterminacyPredicate (falls back to Literal Undef).
Verify arity check passes (should already work from step-2). Fix any issues.

Write test error_non_identifier_argument: compile 'structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm \n let d = determined(x + 1) }'. Assert error diagnostic containing 'bare identifier' or 'identifier'. Assert the let 'd' does NOT compile to DeterminacyPredicate.
Verify non-identifier check passes (should already work from step-2). Fix any issues.

Write test error_unresolved_name: compile 'structure S { let d = determined(nonexistent) }'. Assert error diagnostic about unresolved or unknown identifier 'nonexistent'. Assert the let 'd' does NOT compile to DeterminacyPredicate.
Verify unresolved-name check passes (should already work from step-2). Fix any issues.

Write test collect_value_refs_includes_cell: construct DeterminacyPredicate manually via CompiledExpr::determinacy_predicate(DeterminacyPredicateKind::Determined, ValueCellId::new('S', 'x')), call collect_value_refs(), assert result contains ValueCellId::new('S', 'x').
Verify collect_value_refs passes (should already work from pre-1). Fix any issues.

Write test content_hash_stability_and_uniqueness: build DeterminacyPredicate for (Determined, S.x) twice — assert same content_hash. Build for (Undetermined, S.x) — assert different hash from Determined. Build for (Determined, S.y) — assert different hash from (Determined, S.x).
Verify content hash test passes (should already work from pre-1 constructor). Fix any issues.

Write test not_compiled_as_function_call: compile 'structure S { param x : Scalar = 5mm \n let d = determined(x) }', extract default_expr for 'd', assert it is NOT CompiledExprKind::FunctionCall. This confirms the intrinsic intercept takes priority over stdlib fallthrough. Also verify no walk()-visited nodes contain a FunctionCall with name 'determined'.
Verify not-function-call test passes (should already work from step-2). Fix any issues.

design_decisions.decision: Intercept before arg compilation, not after
design_decisions.rationale: Determinacy predicates need the raw AST argument to verify it's a bare ExprKind::Ident. Compiling the arg first would lose the distinction between 'determined(x)' (valid bare ident) and 'determined(x + 0)' (invalid expression). The some() intercept pattern at line 1437 is the precedent — it intercepts before general overload resolution.

design_decisions.decision: Only support bare identifiers as arguments, not member access expressions
design_decisions.rationale: The scope.resolve() mechanism handles bare names including port composite keys (port.member registered as a single key). Supporting arbitrary MemberAccess (e.g., sub.param) would require walking the AST member access chain, adding significant complexity. The task description says 'resolves the argument to a specific ValueCellId' — bare identifiers are the canonical way to do this in the scope system.

design_decisions.decision: Eval layer (reify-expr) returns Value::Undef for DeterminacyPredicate
design_decisions.rationale: EvalContext only has ValueMap (ValueCellId -> Value), not DeterminacyState. The actual determinacy evaluation happens at the engine level (reify-eval) where Snapshot.values stores (Value, DeterminacyState). This matches the existing pattern where stdlib stubs return Undef and the engine intercepts at a higher level. A future task will add snapshot-level evaluation.

design_decisions.decision: Content hash tag byte is 16 with kind discriminant
design_decisions.rationale: Tags 0-15 are used by existing variants (0=Literal, 1=ValueRef, 2=BinOp, 3=UnOp, 5=Conditional, 7=Lambda, 8=ListLiteral, 9=SetLiteral, 10=MapLiteral, 11=IndexAccess, 12=MethodCall, 13=Quantifier, 14=OptionSome, 15=OptionNone; 4/6 used in compiler). Hash is ContentHash::of(&[16, kind as u8]).combine(cell_id_hash) for uniqueness across kinds and cells.

design_decisions.decision: DeterminacyPredicate is a leaf node in walk() with cell as dependency in collect_value_refs
design_decisions.rationale: The cell ValueCellId is resolved at compile time and stored directly — no child expressions to recurse. The cell IS a dependency (added in collect_value_refs_inner) because the determinacy state of a cell changes when the cell's value changes, so dependents must be re-evaluated.
Determinacy predicates answer a yes/no question about a cell's state (is it determined? is it constrained? etc.). Bool is the natural return type regardless of the cell's own type.

reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: check function name before compiling args, validate arity, inspect raw AST arg kind, emit a specialized CompiledExprKind variant instead of FunctionCall. Insert the determinacy intercept right after the some() check.
Reuse the compile_and_get_expr helper pattern (parse source, compile, extract default_expr for named cell) and compile_expecting_errors helper in the new determinacy_compile_tests.rs.
Follow the same constructor pattern: compute content_hash from tag byte + constituent data, return CompiledExpr { kind, result_type, content_hash }.
Use scope.resolve(arg_name) to get the ValueCellId for the bare identifier argument, exactly as the Ident case does at line 1284.

reuse.what: Diagnostic error pattern with source span
reuse.how: Follow same error reporting pattern: Diagnostic::error(message).with_label(DiagnosticLabel::new(expr.span, label)), then return CompiledExpr::literal(Value::Undef, Type::Bool).
